steps.text: Prepare the "Procurement Request Package".
Submit the "Procurement Request Package" to the Office of Financial Resources (OFR).
Include the requirements and guidance in all procurement request packages, RFPs, and new or renegotiated contracts and/or permits.
Emphasize the purpose of the Food Service Guidelines for Federal Facilities in the Scope of Work language.
Indicate in the Scope of Work that CDC is requesting proposals from experienced food service vendors.
Draft Scope of Work language to emphasize the purpose of the Food Service Guidelines and indicate CDC is requesting proposals from experienced vendors.
Incorporate the Food Service Guidelines standards into the procurement request package, RFP Statement of Work, and contractual/permit performance requirements.
Require the vendor to comply with food and nutrition standards for prepared foods, packaged foods, and beverages at the standard implementation level.
Require the vendor to implement at least one standard from the behavioral design categories at the innovative implementation level.
Require the vendor to comply with the facility efficiency standards (Purchasing Standards, Food Service Management and Consumer Engagement, and Waste Diversion) at the standard implementation level.
Require the vendor to comply with the Food Code food safety standards at the standard level of implementation.
Explicitly require adherence to all applicable state and/or local food safety regulations in the final contract.
Require adherence to all applicable state and/or local food safety regulations.
Require the vendor to produce a reporting plan with monthly sales data and monthly or weekly operating statements in the RFP and subsequent approved contract or permit.
Describe in the plan how the vendor will monitor implementation efforts and outline the methods they will use to track procurement or purchasing patterns for healthier food and beverage items.
Include key indicators in the plan that will be reported to the assigned COR or project officer post award.
Develop a Procurement Request Package, with a Statement of Work, that reflects minimum requirements defined by this policy
Process the Procurement Request Package submitted by the Program Official and take all actions on behalf of the U.S. Government as permitted by the Federal Acquisition Regulations
Determine the adequacy of performance by the vendor in accordance with the terms and conditions of this policy and stipulated in the contract and/or permit
Ensure compliance with requirements through periodic reviews and inspections
Assist the vendor with the resolution of any issues that arise regarding performance or implementation

Assist the Program Official, CO, or assigned COR or Project Officer in integrating Food Service Guidelines requirements, as defined by this policy, into Procurement Request Packages, CDC-issued RFPs, and final approved contracts or permits
Provide technical direction and guidance related to issues that may jeopardize the successful implementation of this policy

preconditions.text: The policy does not apply to existing food service contracts or permits in place prior to adoption of this policy.
The policy does not apply to food and beverages served at CDC employee organization-sponsored events, personal employee-initiated gatherings, or those brought in/paid for by an outside organization.
The policy does not apply to food or light refreshments provided with CDC-appropriated funding.
The policy does not apply to food and beverages sold and served as part of an emergency response or crisis.
Post award of the contract and/or permit

constraints.text: Comply with Executive Order 13990, Protecting Public Health and the Environment and Restoring Science To Tackle the Climate Crisis, and food safety standards in the U.S. Food Code.
Vendors that indicate an ability to implement nutrition standards at the innovative level may be given preference.
Vendors that indicate an ability to implement more than one behavioral design standard at the innovative implementation level may be given preference.
The selected food service vendor must be able to meet all minimum performance requirements, as stipulated by this policy.
